senses.definition: resembles a prologue in style or function; introductory in nature
examples: The book begins with a prologuelike chapter that sets up the main story.
Her speech had a prologuelike quality, briefly introducing the topic before the debate.

senses.definition: a person who composes or performs a prologue; a storyteller or introducer
examples: The prologuer set the scene for the drama with a narrated introduction.
As a prologuer, he often spoke before the main presentation to provide context.

senses.definition: short introductory sections at the beginning of a book or play
examples: The novel includes several prologues that hint at the main mystery.
Some plays begin with prologues spoken by a chorus.

senses.definition: the act of writing or delivering a prologue; introducing information at the start
examples: The author is prologuing the novel with a historical essay.
She spent the afternoon prologuing the lecture with background context.

senses.definition: to create or present a prologue; to introduce with a prefatory section
examples: The author chose to prologuise the story with a short myth.
The speaker prologuized the talk with a quotation from a famous poet.

senses.definition: having been introduced with a prologue; preceded by a prefatory section
examples: The novel is prologuised by an ancient tale that foreshadows events.
A prologuised speech often helps listeners understand the context before the main argument.

senses.definition: a person or thing that prologues or introduces; a prologue introducer
examples: The author acts as the prologuiser, setting the scene for the reader.
In some plays, the prologuiser speaks a few lines to frame the plot.

senses.definition: third-person singular form of prologuise; introduces or prefactors with a prologue
examples: The author prologuises the narrative with a myth.
She prologuises each chapter with a brief preface.

senses.definition: present participle of prologuise; introducing with a prologue
examples: The author is prologuising the tale with a legend.
They are prologuising the lecture to provide historical context.

senses.definition: a person who writes or delivers prologues
examples: The prologuist crafted a haunting opening for the novel.
As a prologuist, she enjoyed setting the tone for the audience.

senses.definition: to write or insert a prologue; to preface with introductory material
examples: The editor decided to prologuize the collection with an introduction.
He prologuizes his speeches to guide listeners into the topic.

senses.definition: having been prologuized; prefaced with an introduction
examples: The prologuized edition includes a director's note at the start.
A prologuized pamphlet can help readers understand the context.

senses.definition: a person who creates prologues; a prologue writer
examples: She earned a reputation as a skilled prologuizer.
The prologuizer's lines set a dramatic mood for the play.

senses.definition: third-person singular form of prologuize; writes or inserts prologues
examples: The editor prologuizes the collection for new readers.
He often prologuizes anthologies with historical notes.

senses.definition: present participle of prologuize; composing or delivering prologues
examples: The author spent weeks prologuizing the trilogy.
Prologuizing the lecture helps the audience follow the argument.

senses.definition: an obscure or historical term possibly referring to a prologue-related concept; limited usage
examples: The manuscript mentions a section called prologulogi, but its meaning is unclear.

senses.definition: a prologue or introductory section; also a genus name in biology (rare)
examples: The scroll begins with a prologus describing the hero's early life.

senses.definition: to make something last longer; to extend in time
examples: We need to prolong our stay to finish the project.
The meeting was prolonged by several questions.

senses.definition: capable of being prolonged or extended
examples: The contract is prolongable if both sides agree.
This battery is prolongable for additional charges.

senses.definition: the quality of being prolongable or able to be extended
examples: Prolongableness is important in warranty terms or rental agreements.

senses.definition: in a manner that can be prolonged; capable of being extended
examples: The schedule can be prolongably adjusted to fit the results.

senses.definition: to extend or lengthen (especially in time)
examples: They aimed to prolongate the discussion to reach a consensus.
Efforts to prolongate the project timeline were unsuccessful.

senses.definition: extended in time; prolonged
examples: The prolongated negotiation took several weeks.
A prolongated period of waiting followed the announcement.

senses.definition: third-person singular of prolongate; extends in time
examples: The agreement prolongates the research period by six months.
Policy changes prolongates the review process.

senses.definition: present participle of prolongate; extending in time
examples: The court is prolongating the case due to new evidence.
